Email complaints to gm@mccbank.in, chairman@mccbank.in with reference number. Call complaint hotline 7090701115 or 9845278901. Online portal: complaints@mccbank.in for digital submission. WhatsApp escalation: 7090701104 with complaint details and reference. RBI Compliance: Complaints must be acknowledged within 1 business day. Resolution target: 7-10 days for standard complaints, 3-5 days for urgent matters. Escalation: If not resolved, complaint can be escalated to RBI Banking Ombudsman. RBI Ombudsman: Free service for customer grievances regarding card services. Ombudsman contact: ombudsmanschemerbi.org.in or nearest RBI branch. Compensation Claims: RBI allows compensation claims for service delays causing financial loss. Dispute Resolution: Card blocking disputes resolved within 30 days per RBI Master Circular. Refunds credited within 10 days of confirmed error by bank. Compensation for fraudulent unblocking or unauthorized blocks per RBI guidelines. Documentation: Keep all correspondence, SMS alerts, complaint reference numbers. Maintain records of resolution timeline and compensation if claimed. Customer Rights: Right to prompt unblocking, Right to grievance redressal, Right to RBI escalation, Right to zero liability for unauthorized transactions. No charge applies for grievance filing or RBI escalation as mandated by RBI regulations.

title: How to Block Your ATM Card - Customer Request Options
content: You can block your ATM/Debit card anytime for security or personal reasons as per RBI guidelines. Blocking Methods: 1. Digital Blocking: Login to mobile app → Settings → Card Management → Select 'Block Card Temporarily' or 'Permanently Block' → Confirm request. Instant blocking within seconds. 2. Net Banking: Visit www.mccbank.in → Card Services → Block ATM Card → Choose duration and reason → Confirm through OTP. 3. Phone: Call 1800-425-1111 anytime 24x7 to request immediate card block. 4. Branch: Visit any MCC Bank branch with ID proof and submit written request for card blocking. 5. WhatsApp: Message 'BLOCK MY CARD' to 7090701104 with card last 4 digits for instant blocking. 6. SMS: Send 'BLOCK' to 9089098000 for OTP-verified mobile. Card Blocking Reasons: Lost or stolen card, Suspicious activity, Travel to high-risk areas, Security concerns, Card expiry soon, Personal security preference, Compromised card details, Unwanted transactions. Temporary vs Permanent Block: Temporary Block: Card remains in system, can be unblocked anytime without replacement, useful for travel or security concerns. Permanent Block: Card cannot be reactivated, replacement card can be requested with fees. Charges: No charge for temporary or permanent card blocking per RBI norms. Replacement card charges apply if permanent block chosen. Processing: Instant blocking through digital channels (mobile app, net banking, WhatsApp). Immediate blocking through phone/IVR within 1 hour. Same-day blocking at branch during business hours. RBI Mandate: Banks must provide multiple card blocking options per Master Circular. No charge or minimal charge applies for card block requests. Zero liability for fraudulent transactions if reported within 30 days. After Blocking: Use alternative payment methods (passbook withdrawal, mobile transfers, cheque). Funds can be transferred through net banking or mobile app. Standing instructions continue for pre-authorized payments. Request unblocking anytime without penalty through same channels.

title: Card Blocking Emergency Situations and Travel Scenarios
content: Block your card immediately in emergency situations as per RBI's Master Circular on Payment Systems. Emergency Blocking: Card Theft or Loss - Block card within 30 minutes through phone 1800-425-1111 to limit fraud liability to Rs. 50,000 per RBI norms. Suspected Fraud - Block card instantly if you see unauthorized transactions through mobile app. Foreign Travel - Temporarily block card if traveling to countries with high fraud rates, reactivate upon return. Compromised PIN/Details - If PIN or CVV shared accidentally, block and request new card with new PIN. Travel Blocking Process: Before International Trip: Call 1800-425-1111 to inform travel dates and countries (phone/SMS to pre-authorized mobile only). Many customers choose temporary card block during high-risk travel. Block Duration: Set end date for automatic unblocking after return date. Or manually unblock through mobile app anytime. Notifications: SMS alert sent immediately when card is blocked. Email confirmation to registered email address. Reference number provided for tracking. ATM Access During Block: Use original passbook for branch withdrawals. Use net banking for transfers. Use mobile wallet if linked to phone. Standing orders continue unaffected. Fraud During Block: If fraud detected before unblocking, report immediately. Zero liability applies if reported within 30 days per RBI. Refund credited within 10 days of confirmed fraud. Unblocking After Travel: Request unblocking through mobile app (instant). Or call customer care 1800-425-1111 (within 1 hour). Automatic unblocking at pre-set date if configured. RBI Compliance: Emergency blocking available 24x7/365 days per regulatory mandate. No charge for emergency blocking per RBI norms. Liability protection covers losses during blocked period if fraud reported promptly.

title: Permanently Block Card and Request Card Closure
content: To permanently block and retire your card at MCC Bank, follow these steps as per card lifecycle management: Permanent Block Process: Step 1: Decision - Confirm you want to permanently close the card (cannot be reversed). Step 2: Digital Request - Submit request through mobile app → Card Management → 'Permanently Close Card'. Step 3: Verification - Provide reason for closure and confirm account details. Step 4: Confirm - Final confirmation required as this action is irreversible. Step 5: Processing - Card permanently blocked within 24 hours. Closure Confirmation - SMS sent with reference number and closure date. Reasons for Permanent Block: Card damaged or worn out, Card expiry soon without renewal need, Security concerns requiring fresh card with new details, Account closure or downgrade, Fraud or unauthorized use, Personal preference to switch payment method. Alternative to Permanent Block: Consider temporary block first if uncertainty exists. Request new card before permanent closure if continuous access needed. Link alternate payment method to avoid service disruption. Replacement Options After Permanent Closure: New debit card issuance - Standard charge Rs. 250 after permanent block (Rush delivery Rs. 500). No reactivation possible once permanently blocked. Processing Timeline: Standard replacement: 7-10 working days. Rush replacement: 1-2 working days available in metros. Funds Access During Closure: Use net banking for transfers and bill payments. Use passbook for branch withdrawals. Use alternative cards or payment methods. Standing instructions work without card. Closure Confirmation: Physical confirmation letter mailed to registered address. Email confirmation sent to registered email. Reference number for tracking status. Branch verification available during business hours. RBI Guidelines: No charge for permanent card closure per regulatory norms. Replacement card charges apply if new card requested. Funds in account unaffected by card closure. Account remains active for deposits and transfers.

title: Fixed Deposit Quick Interest Rate Reference 456 Days
content: MCC Bank Fixed Deposit 456 Days Interest Rates (Effective 02-04-2026): Standard Customer: 7.00% per annum. Senior Citizen: 7.50% per annum (additional 0.50% benefit). Co-operative Societies and Banks: 7.50% per annum for deposits above Rs. 25,00,000 (additional 0.50% benefit). These are the premium rates for 456-day fixed deposits. No lock-in period penalty. Interest compounding available. Premature withdrawal allowed with 1% deduction from applicable rate per RBI norms. Nomination facility available. Automatic renewal option at maturity. Standing instruction available for investments. Minimum deposit: Rs. 100. Maximum deposit: No limit. Processing: Same-day credit after KYC verification. Cheque received date considered as start date. Maturity date calculated from receipt date. Statement available through passbook and mobile app. Tax treatment: Interest income taxable as per applicable slab. TDS applicable if interest exceeds Rs. 40,000 in financial year. Senior citizen exemption available with Form 15G/15H. RBI Master Circular compliance: All rates as per RBI's Deposits Acceptance and Repayment (DAR) guidelines. Highest rates in MCC Bank's product portfolio for deposit products.

title: All Fixed Deposit Tenor Rates and Maturity Benefits
content: MCC Bank Fixed Deposit Complete Interest Rate Chart (Effective 02-04-2026): Short Tenor Deposits: 15 days to 45 days - 4.00% per annum. 46 days to 90 days - 4.75% per annum. 91 days to 180 days - 5.25% per annum. 181 days to 364 days - 6.00% per annum. Medium Tenor Deposits: 1 year to less than 2 years - 6.75% per annum. 2 years and above but less than 5 years - 6.50% per annum. Premium Tenor Deposit: 456 days - 7.00% per annum (highest rate). 456 days Senior Citizen - 7.50% per annum. Flexible Options: Cumulative option: Interest compounded and paid at maturity. Non-cumulative option: Interest paid monthly/quarterly/half-yearly as per choice. Maturity Benefits: Automatic renewal at current rates. Option to transfer to current/savings account. Reinvestment into new FD at prevailing rates. Loan against deposit available up to 85% of deposit amount. Loan interest: 1% higher than deposit rate for Rs. 25 Lakhs and above, 2% higher for amounts below Rs. 25 Lakhs. Senior Citizen Benefits: Additional 0.50% rate benefit across all tenors. Special schemes available for 456-day deposits. Tax exemption under Section 80C if applicable. Minimum Deposit: Rs. 100 for all tenors. No maximum limit per deposit. Joint deposit facility available. Nomination mandatory per RBI guidelines. Premature Closure: Allowed anytime with 1% interest deduction from applicable rate. No penalty in case of death. Automatic maturity renewal at prevailing rates if not withdrawn within 7 days.

title: How to Open Fixed Deposit and Current Rates
content: How to Open MCC Bank Fixed Deposit - Simple Process: Online: Visit www.mccbank.in → Deposits → Open FD → Select tenor and amount → Pay through net banking → Instant FD issuance with receipt. Mobile App: MCC Bank app → My Deposits → New FD → Choose tenor (15 days to 456 days) → Enter amount (minimum Rs. 100) → Authorize with OTP. Branch Visit: Visit any MCC Bank branch with identity proof and deposit amount → Submit FD application form → Receive FD receipt same-day. Documents Required: Identity proof (Aadhaar/PAN/Passport/Driving Licence) - Aadhaar mandatory. Address proof if changed from registered address. Proof of funds for deposits above Rs. 10 lakhs. Required Information: Name and contact details. Account number for fund debit. Nominee details (mandatory per RBI). Maturity instruction preference (renewal, transfer to account, etc.). Current Premium Rates: 456 days FD: 7.00% per annum (Regular) or 7.50% (Senior Citizen). 1 year to 2 years: 6.75% per annum. 2 to 5 years: 6.50% per annum. Interest Payment Options: Cumulative (compound interest at maturity). Monthly interest payout to account. Quarterly interest payout. Half-yearly interest payout. Annual interest payout. Processing: Same-day confirmation after KYC verification. FD receipt issued immediately (digital and physical). Interest accrual begins from next day. Rates applicable from date of issuance. Standing instruction available for automatic monthly investment of FD amounts. Customer Care: Call 1800-425-1111 for current rate queries. Email to info@mccbank.in for bulk FD queries. WhatsApp to 7090701104 for account-linked assistance.

title: Fixed Deposit vs Recurring Deposit Comparison and Benefits
content: MCC Bank Fixed Deposit (FD) vs Recurring Deposit (RD) - Comparison: Fixed Deposit: Lump-sum investment: Invest entire amount upfront in single payment. Flexible tenure: 15 days to 5+ years available. Premium rates: 456 days gives 7.00% (Regular) or 7.50% (Senior Citizen). Fixed return: Interest amount predetermined. Maturity amount = Principal + Interest calculated upfront. Low minimum: Start with just Rs. 100. Premature withdrawal: Allowed with 1% interest deduction. Recurring Deposit: Monthly installments: Invest fixed amount every month. Standard tenure: 6 months to 10 years. Competitive rates: 1-2 years = 6.75%, 2-5 years = 6.50%. Systematic savings: Builds discipline through regular deposits. Flexible amounts: Installments from Rs. 100 per month. Loan facility: Borrow up to 85% of balance after 3 months. When to Choose FD: You have lump sum amount available now. You want guaranteed returns on entire investment. You want highest interest rates (456 days FD = 7.00-7.50%). You need liquidity with premature withdrawal option. Short-term investment goals (15 days to 1 year). When to Choose RD: You prefer monthly installment saving. You want to build disciplined savings habit. You get salary monthly and want to invest part of it. You need loan facility against deposits. You want flexibility in maturity period. RD Rates Currently: 1 year to less than 2 years: 6.75% per annum. 2 years and above but less than 5 years: 6.50% per annum. Senior Citizen Benefit: Additional 0.50% on both FD and RD. Nomination Available: Both FD and RD support mandatory nomination. Tax Treatment: Both interest taxable as per income slab. TDS applicable at 20% if interest exceeds Rs. 40,000 per annum (subject to Form 15G/15H). Senior citizens exempted with valid form. Combination Strategy: Open FD with current surplus for immediate high returns (7.00%). Simultaneously start RD for monthly systematic savings (6.75%). Both mature and reinvest based on needs.

title: How to Open a Savings Account - Complete Step-by-Step Procedure
content: How to Open MCC Bank Savings Account - Complete Procedure: Online (Instant Opening): Step 1: Visit www.mccbank.in or download MCC Bank mobile app. Step 2: Click 'Open Savings Account' → Select account type (Regular/Student/Youth). Step 3: Enter personal details: Name, date of birth, gender, email, mobile number. Step 4: Complete identity verification: Submit Aadhaar/PAN/Passport electronically. Step 5: Verify address through registered address proof or self-declaration. Step 6: Set net banking and mobile banking credentials. Step 7: Initial deposit: Pay Rs. 1000 minimum through net banking or card. Step 8: Receive instant confirmation and account number via SMS/email. Processing: Immediate account opening within 5 minutes. Debit card issued within 7 days to registered address. Cheque book can be requested after account activation. Branch Visit (Traditional Method): Step 1: Visit nearest MCC Bank branch with documents (see below). Step 2: Request Savings Account opening form from counter. Step 3: Fill all details: Name, address, contact, nominee information. Step 4: Submit documents for verification (original + copy). Step 5: Verify identity with KYC officer. Step 6: Provide initial deposit: Minimum Rs. 1000 (cheque/cash). Step 7: Receive acknowledgment receipt and provisional account number. Step 8: Account activation within 24 hours, confirmation SMS sent. Processing: Same-day account opening during branch hours. Passbook and debit card issued within 7 days. Cheque book available on request after Rs. 1000 minimum balance maintained. Documents Required (Mandatory): Aadhaar card (original + copy) - MANDATORY. For identity: Passport/Driving License/Voter ID (any one). For address proof: If different from Aadhaar - Utility bill/Rent agreement/Government ID. PAN card (optional but recommended for TDS exemption). Passport-sized photographs (2 copies). For Joint Account: Both account holders must provide above documents separately. Nominee Information: Name of nominee (mandatory per RBI). Nominee's date of birth. Nominee's relationship with account holder. Nominee's contact address and phone number. Alternate nominee recommended (optional). Account Opening Options: Individual Savings Account: Single person in own name. Joint Savings Account: Two or more individuals jointly. Minor Account: Guardian opens on behalf of minor. Senior Citizen Account: Additional 0.50% interest benefit. Student/Youth Account: For persons below 25 years. Special Benefits: No minimum balance for first 6 months from opening (after which Rs. 1000 minimum required). Free passbook and first cheque book. Free net banking and mobile banking access. Free ATM withdrawals at MCC Bank ATMs (up to 5 per month, then Rs. 20 per withdrawal). Free NEFT and RTGS fund transfers for 6 months. Processing Fees: Zero opening charges per RBI guidelines. No processing or application fees. All documents verified free of cost. Time Required: Online: 5-10 minutes for account opening. Branch: 15-30 minutes for account opening. Cheque book: 5-7 business days from opening date. Debit card: 5-7 business days from opening date. After Opening: Receive account number via SMS within 1 hour. Email with account statement template sent. Debit card and cheque book delivered to registered address. Standing instruction facility available after first successful transaction. Interest calculation begins from next banking day. Contact Support: Call 1800-425-1111 for account opening queries. Visit nearest branch during 9 AM to 4 PM business hours. Email: info@mccbank.in for assistance. WhatsApp: 7090701104 for quick queries.

title: Savings Account Types and Features Comparison
content: MCC Bank Savings Account Types and Features: Regular Savings Account: Interest rate: 3.00% per annum. Minimum balance: Rs. 1000 per quarter. Cheque book facility: Available with Rs. 1000 balance. Eligible for: All individuals, businesses, institutions. Monthly charges if balance falls below minimum: Rs. 100 per quarter. Super Saver Account (High Balance): Minimum balance requirement: Rs. 5,00,000. Interest rate: 6.00% per annum (highest for savings). Special benefits: Waived service charges. Priority customer service. Free investment advisory. Dedicated relationship manager. Youth/Student Savings Account: Age eligibility: Students and youth below 25 years. Minimum opening balance: Rs. 100 only. Minimum balance requirement: Rs. 0 (zero balance account). Interest rate: 4.00% per annum. Special features: Free debit card (annual fee waived for 5 years). Free cheque book with 10 leaves. Unlimited free transactions. No penalty for non-maintenance of balance. Auto-conversion to regular SB on turning 25 years. Senior Citizen Savings Account: Age eligibility: 60 years and above. Minimum opening balance: Rs. 1000. Minimum balance requirement: Rs. 1000 per quarter. Interest rate: 3.50% per annum (additional 0.50% benefit). Cheque book facility: 20 leaves free annually. Priority service: Dedicated senior citizen service desk. Special features: Medical emergency withdrawal facility. Nominee facility emphasized. Family discussion sessions on financial planning. NRE Savings Account (Non-Resident External): Eligible for: Indian citizens residing abroad (NRI). Currency: Foreign currency deposits accepted. Interest rate: As per international market rates (varies). Repatriation: Fully repatriable to foreign country. Minimum balance: Rs. 2000. Special features: Simplified KYC for NRIs. Foreign currency denomination available. NRO Savings Account (Non-Resident Ordinary): Eligible for: NRIs earning local income in India. Currency: Indian Rupees only. Interest rate: 3.00% per annum (same as regular SB). Repatriation: Subject to RBI approval. Minimum balance: Rs. 1000. Special features: Manages locally earned income. Partially repatriable. Selecting Right Account Type: Regular SB: For average customer needs, general transactions. Super Saver: For high networth individuals seeking premium benefits. Student/Youth: For students studying or just starting career. Senior Citizen: For retired professionals and pensioners. NRE/NRO: For Non-Resident Indians managing India connections. Conversion Options: Student account converts automatically to regular SB on age 25. Super Saver account downgradeable to regular SB anytime. NRE/NRO conversion requires RBI approval. Service Charges Apply: Minimum balance not maintained: Rs. 100 per quarter. Cheque book (extra leaves): Rs. 4 per leaf (after 20 free leaves annually). Passbook duplicate: Rs. 20 per page. Account closure within 1 year: Rs. 200. Common Features Across All: Free net banking and mobile banking. SMS alerts for all transactions. NEFT/RTGS facility. Demand draft issuance. Standing instruction support. Loan against savings account (up to 90% after 1 year). Overdraft facility available.

title: Documents Required for Savings Account Opening - Complete List
content: MCC Bank Savings Account - Documents Required for Opening: MANDATORY Documents (All Applicants Must Provide): Aadhaar Card: Original + Self-attested photocopy. Aadhar is COMPULSORY per RBI KYC norms. Proof from any of these if address has changed from Aadhaar: Passport (original + copy). Driving License (original + copy). Voter ID (original + copy). PAN Card: Original or self-attested photocopy (recommended for tax benefits). Passport-sized Photographs: 2 copies (4cm x 6cm size). Signature Specimen: On account opening form (bank provides specimen for reference). Contact Information: Valid mobile number (SMS alerts registered). Email address (for e-statements). For Joint Accounts (Additional Documents): Both account holders must provide same mandatory documents separately. Joint account application form signed by both parties. Clear indication of survivorship clause preferences. For Minor Accounts (Guardian Opens): Minor's Aadhaar/Birth certificate. Guardian's full Aadhaar card + copy. Guardian's PAN card. Guardianship certificate (if court-appointed). Guardian's identity and address proof. Minor's 2 passport-sized photographs. For Corporate Accounts (Clubs, Societies, Trusts, Associations): Certificate of Incorporation or Registration. Partnership deed (for partnerships). Bylaws or Constitution document. Board Resolution authorizing account opening. Identity proof of authorized signatory. PAN certificate of entity. Address proof of registered office. For NRE Account (Non-Resident External): Valid Passport (original + copy). Visa copy of country of residence. Bank certificate confirming NRI status. Address proof in foreign country. PAN card (if available). Employer letter confirming salary/income abroad. For NRO Account (Non-Resident Ordinary): Valid Passport (original + copy). Address proof in foreign country. PAN card. Indian address for local income sources. Proof of local income (salary slip/business registration). Original or self-attested copies acceptable: Banks accept either original verified at branch or self-attested copies. Self-attestation must be on each page with signature. PAN is NOT compulsory but highly recommended: Provides tax exemption on interest earned. Enables TDS exemption if aggregate interest is below Rs. 40,000/year. Processing Timeline: All documents verified same-day during branch visit. Account opening completed within 24 hours. Passbook and debit card delivered within 7 days. Verification Process: KYC officer verifies all documents against originals. Photographs checked for quality and clarity. Signature specimen compared with bank records. Address proof validated for current residence. Account opening form checked for completeness. What documents are NOT required: Birth certificate (unless for minor account). Ration card (not accepted as valid proof). School certificates. Medical certificates. Employer letters (unless for NRI account). Alternate valid documents if originals not available: Old PAN document + new PAN certificate. Expired passport + renewed passport. Earlier bank account statement showing address. Utility bill from registered postal address. Government employee ID card. Fee for Account Opening: Zero opening charges (per RBI guidelines). No document verification charges. Free service. Submission Process: Submit all documents together with account opening form. Bank officer will guide you through the process. Keep photocopies for your records. Request acknowledgment receipt after submission.

title: Documents Required for Fixed and Recurring Deposits
content: MCC Bank Fixed Deposit (FD) and Recurring Deposit (RD) - Documents Required: FOR INDIVIDUAL DEPOSITORS: Aadhaar Card: Original + Self-attested copy (MANDATORY). PAN Card: Original or self-attested copy (Recommended). Identity Proof (any one): Passport, Driving License, Voter ID card. Address Proof (if different from Aadhaar): Passport, Utility bill, Rent agreement, Government ID. Passport-sized Photographs: 1-2 copies. Signature Specimen: On deposit receipt/form. Cheque for initial deposit amount (post-dated or current dated). FOR JOINT DEPOSITS: Both depositors provide separate mandatory documents. Additional jointly signed document acknowledging survivorship clause. Each depositor provides signature specimen. FOR DEPOSITS ON BEHALF OF MINOR: Guardian's Aadhaar + identity proof. Guardian's PAN card. Minor's Aadhaar or Birth certificate. Court-appointed guardian certificate (if applicable). Guardianship letter on letterhead. Minor's photograph. FOR CORPORATE/BUSINESS DEPOSITS: Business registration certificate. Certificate of Incorporation (for companies). Partnership deed (for partnerships). Board Resolution authorizing deposit. Signatory authorization letter. PAN certificate of business entity. Address proof of registered office. Signatory's Aadhaar + PAN. FOR NRI/NRE DEPOSITS: Valid Passport (original + copy). Visa of country of residence. Bank certificate confirming NRI status. Foreign address proof. PAN card (if available). Employer letter confirming overseas income. Repatriation permission letter (for NRE deposits). FOR SENIOR CITIZENS: Aadhaar + age proof (birth certificate/passport). PAN card. Identity and address proof. Medical certificate (if requested for special benefits). Processing: All documents collected at time of deposit. Same-day verification by bank officer. Deposit receipt issued with reference number. Charges: Zero charges for document verification. Minimum FD Deposit: Rs. 100 (with any valid identity). Minimum RD Installment: Rs. 100 per month. Online Deposits (Digital): For existing MCC Bank account holders: Aadhaar verification (already on file). Net banking credentials. Mobile app registration. Identity re-verification may be required for deposits above Rs. 10 lakhs. Digital Signature on e-forms. e-KYC completion through video verification. For new customers opening FD online: Complete e-KYC process through OTP verification. Aadhaar e-sign authentication. Video verification with bank officer. Self-attested document upload. Important Notes: Original documents shown at branch, photocopies retained by bank. Aadhaar mandatory for all deposits per RBI norms. PAN beneficial for senior citizens and high-interest earners. Joint deposits require both parties to be present or provide Power of Attorney. Senior citizen deposits eligibility: Age 60+ with valid age proof. Additional 0.50% interest benefit with age certificate. Deposits above Rs. 10 lakhs require additional verification. Bulk deposits may require business registration and compliance documents.

title: Documents Required for Opening Current Account
content: MCC Bank Current Account - Documents Required for Opening: FOR SOLE PROPRIETORS: Aadhaar Card: Original + Self-attested copy (MANDATORY). PAN Card: Original + copy. Business registration certificate or Trade License. Proof of business existence (1-year business registration, GST certificate, shop act license). Business address proof: Utility bill, Rent deed, Factory permit, Lease agreement. Personal address proof: Passport, Driving License, Voter ID. Passport-sized photographs: 2 copies. Bank reference letter from previous banker (if switching). FOR PARTNERSHIP FIRMS: Partnership Deed: Original + certified copy. PAN Certificate of the firm. GST registration certificate. Proof of business: Shop Act license, Import-Export code, Factory license. Authorized partner's Aadhaar + PAN card. All partners' identity and address proof. Business address proof. Partnership authorization letter specifying signatories. FOR PRIVATE LIMITED COMPANIES: Certificate of Incorporation. Memorandum and Articles of Association. Board Resolution authorizing current account opening. List of authorized signatories with specimen signatures. PAN Certificate of company. GST registration certificate. Office address proof: Utility bill, Rent deed, Lease agreement. Directors' Aadhaar + PAN cards. Company seal impression. FOR LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIPS (LLP): LLP Registration certificate. Deed of LLP. PAN certificate of LLP. GST registration. Authorized partner's Aadhaar + PAN. Business address proof. Authorization document. FOR NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS (NGO/Trust/Society): Society/Trust registration certificate (from Charity Commissioner). Bylaws/Constitution of organization. PAN certificate. NGO registration certificate (if applicable). Address proof of office. Authorized signatory's Aadhaar + PAN. Board Resolution or General Body Resolution. List of office-bearers/Board members. FOR BANKS AND FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS: RBI authorization/license copy. Certificate of Incorporation. PAN certificate. GST registration certificate. Board Resolution. Authorized signatories' documents. Branches require proof of principal office. Processing Documents: All documents submitted in original + 2 attested copies. Bank officer verifies documents against originals. Certification by gazetted officer may be required for some documents. Processing Time: 3-5 working days for verification. Account activated within 7 days after document approval. Confirmatory call made before account activation. Minimum Opening Balance: Rs. 5,000 (varies by business type). Charges for Account Opening: Zero opening charges (per RBI guidelines). Charges Apply After Opening: Monthly charges if minimum balance not maintained. Quarterly fees for specific transaction types. Annual statement charges. Overdraft processing fees. Additional Required Documents: Latest ITR (Income Tax Return) for 1-3 years. Audited financial statements (for companies/partnerships). List of current bank accounts and loans. Credit report authorization. Business insurance policy copy. For new businesses: Business plan, registration certificate, promoter details. For established businesses: Annual reports, balance sheet, profit/loss statement. Special Considerations: Existing MCC Bank account holders may need fewer documents. NRI opening current accounts require additional NRI documentation. Corporate customers may need board resolutions. Government/PSU accounts require special authorization. Changes to authorized signatories require Board Resolution and updated documents.

title: Documents Required for All Loan Types - Complete Reference
content: MCC Bank Loans - Documents Required by Loan Type: COMMON DOCUMENTS FOR ALL LOANS: Applicant's Aadhaar Card: Original + Self-attested copy (MANDATORY). PAN Card: Original + copy. Identity Proof: Passport, Driving License, Voter ID (any one). Address Proof: Utility bill, Rent agreement, Passport (latest). Passport-sized Photographs: 2-3 copies. Bank statements: Last 12 months from existing account. Form 16/ITR: Last 3 years' income tax returns. Salary Certificate: From current employer (if salaried). FOR EDUCATION LOAN: Student's Aadhaar + PAN. Parent's/Guardian's documents (as above). School/College admission letter. Course curriculum and fee structure. Bank statements showing capacity to pay remaining expenses. Age proof (birth certificate/school certificate). Guarantor documents: Aadhaar + PAN + identity proof. Collateral security documents if applicable. FOR HOME LOAN (MORTGAGE LOAN): Property documents: Sale deed, Possession letter. Property tax receipt (latest). Building plan approval from local authority. Encumbrance certificate (7-10 years). Property insurance policy copy. Spouse's consent letter (if property is self-acquired before marriage). Title deed (attested by lawyer). Valuation report from bank-approved valuers. FOR GOLD LOAN: Gold ornaments (physical inspection required). Assay certificate (if already tested). Gold valuation report from bank. Insurance policy on gold. Aadhaar + PAN (minimum documents). Signature specimen on loan agreement. FOR BUSINESS LOAN (MSME/CCL): Business registration certificate. GST registration certificate. Audited balance sheet (last 3 years). Profit/Loss statement (last 12 months). Bank statements (last 12 months). Income Tax Returns (last 3 years). Business address proof. Utility bills (business premises). Income tax assessment order (AY). Business license/Shop Act license. For new businesses: Business plan with projections, Certificate of incorporation, Promoter background. FOR VEHICLE LOAN: Vehicle purchase invoice or quotation. Insurance quote or existing policy. Driving license (photocopy). Registration proof if existing vehicle. Valuation report. Hypothecation agreement. FOR PERSONAL LOAN/CONSUMER LOAN: Last 12 months bank statements. Salary slips (last 3 months). Employment letter confirming position and salary. ITR copies (last 2 years). Aadhaar + PAN (as above). Guarantor documents (if required). FOR AGRICULTURE LOAN: Land ownership documents (Patta/Chitta/Record of Rights). Aadhaar + PAN (farmer). Agricultural insurance certificate. Farming license (if applicable). Revenue receipts (land tax paid proof). Soil test report (for crop loans). Crop insurance details. FOR TRANSPORT LOAN (Vehicle Purchase): Vehicle proforma invoice/Quotation. Insurance quotation. DL photocopy. Company registration certificate (if company owns). Bank statements (last 6 months). Income tax returns (last 2 years). For commercial vehicles: SADO license, Fitness certificate (old vehicles). FOR SURETY/CONSUMER LOAN: Applicant documents (as above). Guarantor's complete documents (as above). Item quotation for purchase. Hypothecation agreement. Insurance proposal (if required). FOR LOAN AGAINST DEPOSIT: Deposit receipt. Passbook. Insurance nomination certificate. Loan application form. Signature specimen. FOR LOAN AGAINST SECURITIES: Security certificate/Share certificate. Demat statement (if electronic). Insurance policy copy. PAN of security owner. Loan application. Additional Documents Often Required: CIBIL score authorization (consent form). Credit report from other banks (credit card statements). Proof of other liabilities (existing loan statements). Court decree (if applicable). Notarized documents (for larger loans). Legal opinion for mortgage/collateral. Valuation certificate from MSTC/approved valuer. Insurance certificates (life/health/property). DIN number (for proprietors/partners). CIN (Company Identification Number). FOR GUARANTOR/SURETY: All documents same as applicant. Additional undertaking letter. Property documents (if personal guarantee secured by property). Proof of financial capacity to guarantee. Loan Agreement Execution: After document verification, loan agreement signed. Hypothecation/Mortgage registered as applicable. Insurance taken on collateral. Funds disbursed within 24-48 hours of document verification completion.
